title: Generative AI Guidelines
summary: SEB is built on trust and relationships, always prioritizing the big picture, understanding client’s needs, and offering the best long-term advice. Clients value this and are willing to pay a premium for it.

columns.title: What is Generative AI?
columns.content: Based on training data, Generative AI mimics human creativity to create new content, such as text, images, music, and more.

sections.section-content: > ## Content created with generative AI must follow SEB’s brand and tone guidelines to maintain consistency.

sections.title: Whenever using AI-generated content, it must:

columns.content:
- Be reviewed by colleagues, relevant Heads, risk managers, or legal when uncertain.
- Undergo fact-checking to ensure accuracy, especially for guides or checklists.
- Comply with all legal and regulatory requirements.

### Disclosure

Any content generated or adjusted by AI must include a clear disclosure, as required by the EU AI Act. This applies to all formats (text, images, audio, video) and must be visible at the first interaction or exposure.

columns.title: Text generation
columns.content:
- Always follow SEB’s verbal identity, even for AI-generated content.
- Avoid uploading non-public text to public AI tools.
- Ensure AI-generated text is reviewed by a human editor for tone, accuracy, facts, and compliance.

columns.title: Image Generation
columns.content:
- AI is not to be used for creating images for any client-facing communication except with approval from the Global Creative Director.
- Exceptions (e.g., image extension or montage) must maintain the integrity of the original image and comply with copyright rules.
- Any AI tools used must guarantee no copyright infringement.
